cost_model.rationale: 5 bps is the initial candidate (conservative all-in spread + impact for the most liquid US ETFs), explicitly NOT final approval; every scenario-grid cell runs at all four sensitivity levels so threshold conformance can be judged across the cost envelope

fallback_proxy_policy.pre_inception_rule: before an instrument's first price date (DBC 2006-02-06 is the binding case), drop it from the quadrant row and renormalize the remaining weights proportionally - mirrors the axis_score reduced-coverage renormalization; runs using a renormalized sleeve are labeled reduced_sleeve
fallback_proxy_policy.missing_day_rule: a missing adj_close on a scheduled trade date defers the trade to the next session with data; no interpolation, no synthetic prices
fallback_proxy_policy.substitute_rule: substitutes are listed for transparency only; actually switching any instrument requires a new sleeve proposal revision and quant_owner sign-off

survivorship_and_data_quality.survivorship_note: the sleeve consists of currently-listed ETFs chosen ex-post; this is acceptable ONLY because the sleeve measures the decision path's regime-switching behavior, not instrument selection skill - the bias is acknowledged and does not transfer to any production allocation claim
checks: no gap of more than 3 consecutive trading days per instrument inside its coverage window (flag, do not interpolate)
adjusted-vs-raw close consistency across split/dividend dates (adj series monotone-consistent with split_factor/div_cash)
zero-volume runs longer than 5 sessions flagged for review
every backtest run must emit a data_quality section listing triggered flags; any triggered flag marks the affected cells reduced_quality

instruments.note: IEF is absent from eod_prices; TLT expresses the deflation-bust duration exposure and has full history

constraint_baselines.note: calibration parameter_grid defines only *_delta_pp offsets (risk_cap_delta_pp, defensive_floor_delta_pp); these are the missing numeric baselines those deltas apply to. Every quadrant row above satisfies both constraints (risk assets max 0.60, defensive min 0.30).

rebalance_policy.cadence: monthly, month-end decision date, latest PIT quadrant
rebalance_policy.trade_trigger: quadrant change OR any weight drift > 0.05 from target
rebalance_policy.note: matches scenario_grid.json monthly cadence assumption; drift band limits turnover between quadrant flips

purpose: reference portfolio for MEASURING turnover/drawdown/volatility/stress/OOS of the quadrant decision path. This sleeve is ONLY the harness measurement instrument: it is NOT a productive allocation, NOT the official allocator, and its existence grants no allocation authority - the official open_macro_v03 output is the quadrant/tilt signal consumed by the allocator (separate repo, out of scope, allocator_publish=false).
